prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for furan (C4H4O, 9 atoms) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fit RI) in PySCF. Gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the neutral; cation charge +1 / multiplicity 2; anion charge −1 / multiplicity 2. Geometry: user-supplied furan.xyz, used as-is (no re-optimization at DFT). Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). All three SCF states (N, N±1) converged. Integrity trustworthy: true; sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. Negative Fukui values on O and H atoms are a known Mulliken artifact with diffuse basis functions; relative rankings among carbon atoms are meaningful. The headline value reported is the maximum f+ (most electrophilic site): f+(C5) = 1.9242931 at atom 5 (C, α-carbon). The most nucleophilic site is also C5 with f−= 0.1616371. The dual descriptor peaks at C5 (Δf = 1.76266), consistent with furan's known preference for electrophilic aromatic substitution at the α-positions (C2/C5). No engine warnings were reported; all integrity checks passed.
